title: RequirementSchema
description: Defines the schema to exchange requirements related information. [req("exchange.requirements.schema")]

origin.description: Optional base origin of the requirements in this entry. e.g. specific branch or commit from a git repository

properties.description:
Optional properties related to all requirements in this entry.

**Note:** If a requirement sets a property key directly, the value set at the requirement will be taken.

Requirement.description: This struct defines the information *mantra* stores about a requirement. [req("req")]

deprecated.description:
true: Marks the requirement to be deprecated.

**Note:** All potential children of such a requirement are also marked as deprecated. [req("req.deprecated")]

ignore.description:
true: Instructs mantra to ignore the requirement for the product it is mapped to.

**Note:** All potential children of such a requirement will also be ignored. [req("req.ignore")]

manual_verification.description:
true: Marks the requirement to require manual verification.

**Note:** All potential children of such a requirement are also marked to require manual verification. [req("req.manual")]

optional.description:
true: Instructs mantra to treat the requirement for the product as optional.

**Note:** All potential children of such a requirement are also marked as optional. [req("req.ignore")]

origin.description: Origin where the requirement is defined at. [req("req.origin")]

parents.description: Optional list of parent requirement IDs. [req("req.hierarchy.mult_parents")]

properties.description: List of custom properties of a requirement. [req("req.properties")]

RequirementPk.description: This struct defines the primary key to identify requirements for a product.

product_id.description: ID of the product the parent requirement is defined in. If `None`, the parent is assumed to be defined in the same product as the child requirement.
